nameMeanings.name: Kilpatrick
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Ulster): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Giolla Phádraig ‘son of the servant of (Saint) Patrick’ (Irish Pádraig). See also Fitzpatrick .
Scottish: habitational name from any of various places called in Gaelic as cill Padraig ‘church of (Saint) Patrick’. See also Kirkpatrick .

variants.name: Kirkpatrick
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from either of two places in Dumfriess called Kirkpatrick or any of various other places in Scotland, all so named from the dedication of their church to Saint Patrick. The Scottish word kirk sometimes replaced Gaelic cill ‘church’ in placenames; compare Kilpatrick .

variants.name: Fitzpatrick
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Declan, Ronan, Liam, Seamus, Sinead, Siobhan, Aileen, Bridie, Brigid, Conan.

Irish: occasionally this may be a genuine Anglo-Norman French patronymic meaning ‘son of Patrick ’, but more often it has been adopted as an Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Giolla Pádraig, ‘son of the servant (i.e. devotee) of (Saint) Patrick’ (see Kilpatrick ).

variants.name: Gilpatrick
meanings: Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Giolla Phádraig ‘son of the servant of (Saint) Patrick’ (see Kilpatrick ).

nameMeanings.name: Isobell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Isabel
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

en.lifeSummary: Isobell Kilpatrick was born in 1190, in Luss, Dunbartonshire, Scotland. She married Lord Umfridus first Colquhoun Kilpatrick in 1218, in Luss, Dunbartonshire, Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1220, in Dunbartonshire, Scotland, at the age of 30.

ko.Luss, Argyll and Bute, Scotland: 영국 스코틀랜드 아그이 앤드 부트 루스
ko.lifeSummary: Isobell Kilpatrick(은)는 1190년 스컷랜드 던바토실르 루스에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1218년 영국 스코틀랜드 던바토실르 루스에서 Lord Umfridus first Colquhoun Kilpatrick(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1220년 스컷랜드 던바토실르에서 30세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Isobell Kilpatrick родилась в 1190 г. в местности лусс, дунбартоншир, Шотландия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Lord Umfridus first Colquhoun Kilpatrick в 1218 г. в местности лусс, дунбартоншир, Шотландия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в 1220 г. в местности дунбартоншир, Шотландия в возрасте 30 лет.
